expectations.text: Identifies TAA under 22 CFR Part 124 as the correct instrument for sharing technical data
expectations.evidence: Opening section states 'A Technical Assistance Agreement (TAA) under 22 CFR Part 124 is the correct authorisation instrument when sharing ITAR-controlled technical data with a foreign person.'

expectations.text: Lists mandatory TAA clauses: retransfer prohibition, US government rights, audit rights, 5-year record retention
expectations.evidence: Required TAA Clauses section lists retransfer prohibition, US government rights, audit rights, and 'Record retention: All records related to the TAA must be retained for 5 years' as mandatory elements under 22 CFR § 124.9.

expectations.text: Explains D-Trade portal submission and 30-60 day DDTC approval timeline
expectations.evidence: Applying for DDTC Approval section states 'Submit via the D-Trade portal (dtrade.pmddtc.state.gov)' and 'Standard processing time: 30–60 days.'

expectations.text: Notes that certain defense articles may require Congressional notification for significant transfers
expectations.evidence: India-Specific Considerations section states 'the transaction may require congressional notification under 22 USC § 2776, which adds a mandatory 15–30 day review period before DDTC can approve.'

expectations.text: Mentions India-specific considerations: India is a major defence partner but not treaty-exempt; licence required
expectations.evidence: India-Specific section states India is a 'Major Defense Partner' but 'is not covered by the Defence Trade Cooperation Treaty exemptions that apply to Australia and the UK (22 CFR §§ 126.6–126.7)' and 'A full DDTC-approved TAA is required.'
